[2018-05-23 21:05] phin8459 (Phin): its just boring that most games first 2 turns are DD DD
[2018-05-23 21:09] elyot: @Phin We actually like that most, but not all, games start with DD/DD
[2018-05-23 21:09] elyot: it feels like the right amount of balance between easy and tough decisions early on
[2018-05-23 21:10] elyot: like if it was 99% it would be bad, if it was 0% it would be bad, but it's probably 80% and that seems OK
[2018-05-23 21:14] elyot: @Drevoed Games like Magic need to balance for 20-person friday night tourneys so that new players can win games, but we have online matchmaking so we don't have that as a design requirement. Our goal is to make a game with the highest possible skill ceiling. With friends who play dozens of games, usually one just teaches the other until end up near the same skill level. Prismata isn't really the type of game where you should expect to have a balanced competitive game with somebody who's just playing for the first time.
[2018-05-23 21:15] elyot: The best we can do is offer handicaps or allow players to make their own (e.g. not working with drones for a turn, etc.)
[2018-05-23 21:18] p0lari (Polari): are there currently plans for event time rotation?

[2018-05-24 11:19] elyot: @Greyman We try to find ways of rewarding our supporters without compromising the competitive experience, and this is one such way. We've not really had any complaints that it was unfair, though I also don't really know if it contributes to any sales

[2018-05-24 12:51] supergreyman (Greyman): I personally didn't consider buying Engineer tier for having more skill cards; they've never come up in my mind. I think a lot of people don't realize it because: 1) This type of information isn't explicitly mentioned in the shop, 2) People generally have been preaching that the bundle contains only "cosmetics and single-player content" without mentioning the gameplay value of the golden/platinum ticket.

This makes me wonder about another question: How many paying customers actually bought the bundle for more skill cards? I know at least 1 player who was a bit upset when he found out about this, but it's not a major concern for most people.

[2018-05-28 22:57] elyot: @Aka There are 2 types of games... ones that run locally and allow undo (missions, analysis), and ones that can be observed and generate replays but do not allow undo past turn boundary
[2018-05-28 22:59] elyot: vs computer is the latter type
[2018-05-28 23:06] elyot: @Masne98888 If people really care so much about Prismata skins that they want to grind rigged matches against our bots 3 times a day for them, I don't really think it's a high priority for us to stop them
[2018-05-28 23:07] elyot: If we had tradeable items or the game was pay-to-win, it would be a different matter, but our collectibles are just supposed to be a fun reward for our players, I don't think it's important for us to take a restrictive attitude toward people who find a way to earn them more easily by making the game boring for themselves
[2018-05-28 23:15] wilson0862 (Wilson): Any thoughts on rating inflation and how it affects the ladder? 2K would be #28ish right now. Anecdotally, this is the very low for 2K historically. I don't know shit about rating ecosystems.
[2018-05-28 23:17] elyot: I expect a bit more inflation before it fully settles out tbh
[2018-05-28 23:18] elyot: basically there was a long period where people got better without a large number of new people entering the system, so alpha players were underrated relative to the casual match folks who are currently getting started with ranked
[2018-05-28 23:18] elyot: quite dramatically in fact
[2018-05-28 23:20] elyot: Another way to think about it is, there was strong *deflation* in 2015-2017 where a 1900-player in early 2015 might only be 1700 now because people just got so much better over those 3 years
[2018-05-28 23:20] elyot: and now a lot of new players are entering, which undoes the deflation and brings that 1700 player back up to 1900

@Phin It's a bit tricky because of some units like drake, vore, zemora. Everyone has an idea of which units they would "want" to get autoattcked. We actually had a test implementation of it and we thought it was a little clunky. We might try again in the future though if it's something that a lot of people want. Not sure about @Masne98888's idea of an even more convoluted/advanced auto-click.
@winterfroststorm I think it might be a cookie thing? Web client should load your options regarding display preferences, but maybe it doesn't work if you play in Steam instead of web.
@Rando We've thought a lot about player-created puzzles, and every game that's tried to do them (portal 2, crashmo, etc.) has ended up with a lot of garbage content and the most successful stuff is just memes.
